title: Flaky Canadian Garlic Poutine
description: A twist on the classic Canadian comfort food, featuring a flaky macrobiotic-friendly crust and a garlicky, cheesy sauce.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine 00 flour, salt, sugar, and active dry yeast.
Add chilled butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work the butter into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add milk and water, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a shaggy mass.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Form the dough into a ball and place it in a lightly oiled bowl, turning to coat evenly.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight.
Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and use a pastry cutter or a knife to cut the dough into 1-inch wide strips.
Bake the dough strips in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the dough is baking, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, for 4-5 minutes, or until the garlic is lightly golden and fragrant.
Remove the garlic butter from the heat and stir in the grated cheddar and mozzarella cheese until smooth.
Remove the baked dough strips from the oven and sprinkle with the garlicky cheese sauce.
Return the baking sheet to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the poutine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving
